title: Bitcoin: A Peer-to-Peer Electronic Cash System
author: Satoshi Nakamoto
note: Primary design paper for Bitcoin's peer-to-peer cash model, proof of work, incentives, and issuance analogy to gold mining.

title: Bitcoin Core source code
author: Bitcoin Core contributors
note: Primary implementation reference for Bitcoin consensus behavior; pin to a release or commit before using for final line-level citations.

title: Money and the Mechanism of Exchange
author: William Stanley Jevons
note: Classic treatment of monetary functions, exchange frictions, metallic money, and historical monetary standards.

title: On the Origin of Money
author: Carl Menger
note: Historical monetary theory source on money as an emergent institution and the saleability of commodities.

title: Barter and Economic Disintegration
author: Caroline Humphrey
note: Anthropological critique useful for avoiding a simple barter-origin story of money.

title: Debt: The First 5,000 Years
author: David Graeber
note: Anthropological and historical critique of simple barter-first narratives; use carefully alongside monetary economics sources.

title: Bitcoin Developer Guide: Block Chain
author: Bitcoin Project contributors
note: Technical guide to Bitcoin's block chain structure, proof of work, confirmations, forks, UTXOs, and validation.

title: Bitcoin Developer Guide: Wallets
author: Bitcoin Project contributors
note: Technical guide to Bitcoin wallets, private keys, addresses, backups, and spending control.

title: Securing your wallet
author: Bitcoin Project contributors
note: User-facing Bitcoin wallet security guidance; useful for custody risk but not sufficient for legal custody claims.

title: A glossary of terms used in payments and settlement systems
author: Committee on Payment and Settlement Systems
note: Institutional terminology source for payment, clearing, settlement, finality, netting, and related systems concepts.

title: Principles for financial market infrastructures
author: Committee on Payment and Settlement Systems and Technical Committee of IOSCO
publisher: Bank for International Settlements and IOSCO
note: Institutional source on payment systems, securities settlement, central counterparties, settlement finality, custody risk, and central-bank oversight.

title: Interpretive Letter 1170: Authority of a National Bank to Provide Cryptocurrency Custody Services for Customers
author: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
publisher: Office of the Comptroller of the Currency
note: U.S. banking-law source on cryptocurrency custody by national banks; use with jurisdictional caution.

title: Bitcoin and Cryptocurrency Technologies
author: Arvind Narayanan, Joseph Bonneau, Edward Felten, Andrew Miller, Steven Goldfeder
note: Technical and academic synthesis on Bitcoin mechanics, consensus, incentives, privacy, wallets, and security assumptions.

title: Mastering Bitcoin: Programming the Open Blockchain, Third Edition
author: Andreas M. Antonopoulos and David A. Harding
note: Open technical book on Bitcoin transactions, keys, wallets, scripts, blocks, mining, and network operation.

title: How Writing Came About
author: Denise Schmandt-Besserat
note: Historical source on tokens, accounting, and the emergence of writing; useful for ledger history after human review.

title: The Nature of Money
author: Geoffrey Ingham
note: Monetary sociology source on money, credit, institutions, and trust; useful as a competing interpretation alongside commodity-money accounts.

title: The Fed Explained: What the Central Bank Does
author: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
publisher: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
note: Institutional overview of Federal Reserve functions; useful for central-bank scope with U.S.-specific limits.

title: What is legal tender?
author: Bank of England
note: Institutional explainer on the narrow meaning of legal tender in the United Kingdom.

title: Legal Tender Guidelines
author: The Royal Mint
note: Institutional guidance on UK legal tender limits; useful for correcting common overstatements.

title: 31 U.S. Code Section 5103 - Legal tender
author: United States Congress
publisher: Legal Information Institute, Cornell Law School
note: U.S. statutory legal-tender text; use only for jurisdiction-specific claims.
